questions.stem: For the translational mechanical system with masses M1 and M2 connected by spring K1, spring K2, and damper B (M1 grounded via K1, M2 grounded via K2, B between M1 and M2, force f(t) applied to M1): Write the differential equations governing the dynamic behavior of the system.
common_errors: Wrong sign on damper coupling term B(x1_dot - x2_dot) â€” missing the relative velocity coupling
Forgetting K2*x2 ground spring on M2 equation
Applying force f(t) to wrong mass
questions.solution_method: Apply Newton's second law to each mass separately. For M1: M1*x1'' + B*(x1' - x2') + K1*x1 = f(t). For M2: M2*x2'' + B*(x2' - x1') + K2*x2 = 0. The damper B couples the two equations through relative velocity.
key_formulas: M*x'' + B*(x1' - x2') + K*x = F (Newton's law for coupled translational system)

questions.stem: For the same translational mechanical system with M1=M2=1 kg, K1=K2=1 N/m, B=1 N-s/m, find the transfer function G(s) = X2(s)/F(s).
common_errors: Sign error in Cramer's rule numerator determinant
Forgetting to negate the off-diagonal term when computing X2 via Cramer's rule
questions.solution_method: Take Laplace transform of both ODEs, substitute numerical values to get (s^2+s+1)*X1 - s*X2 = F and -s*X1 + (s^2+s+1)*X2 = 0. Solve for X2 using Cramer's rule: X2 = -s*F / [(s^2+s+1)^2 - s^2]. Expand denominator: s^4+2s^3+2s^2+2s+1. Result: G(s) = s / (s^4+2s^3+2s^2+2s+1).
key_formulas: Cramer's rule: X2 = det([A11, F; A21, 0]) / det(A)

questions.stem: For the same translational mechanical system (M1, M2, K1, K2, B), draw the equivalent electrical analogous circuit using the force-voltage (F-V) analogy.
common_errors: Confusing F-V vs F-I analogy (massâ†”inductor vs massâ†”capacitor)
Incorrect topology â€” K (spring) maps to capacitor 1/K in series loop, not parallel
Missing the shared damper branch between the two mesh loops
questions.solution_method: Apply F-V analogy: forceâ†”voltage, velocityâ†”current, mass Mâ†”inductor L, damper Bâ†”resistor R, spring Kâ†”capacitor 1/K. Draw two series RLC mesh loops sharing the damper resistor B. Loop 1: voltage source f(t), inductor M1, capacitor 1/K1, shared resistor B. Loop 2: inductor M2, capacitor 1/K2, shared resistor B.
key_formulas: F-V analogy: Mâ†”L, Bâ†”R, Kâ†”1/C (springâ†”capacitor with value 1/K)

notes: Single-question quiz (Q1 with three sub-parts a/b/c), MM=6, Time=12 minutes, Date 27.01.2026. Entire quiz focuses on translational mechanical modelling and F-V analogy â€” purely Module 2 content. Sub-part (c) solution is diagram-only in the PDF.
